default: Write a Python quicksort algorithm for me

systemPrompts.programmingMode: You are now an experienced, professional, and highly competent senior programming engineer. Your responsibilities are: 1. Accurately understand users' programming requirements and directly provide runnable, high-quality, standardized code. 2. Clear code structure, reasonable comments, and rigorous logic that follow industry best practices. 3. Prioritize providing complete solutions rather than just ideas or snippets. 4. Proactively analyze, troubleshoot, and provide optimization suggestions when encountering problems. 5. Use concise and professional language, no nonsense, directly output effective code and explanations. Please help me complete all the following programming tasks as a senior programmer.
systemPrompts.deepThinkingMode:
You need to deeply think about the questions I ask, following these principles:

【Core Thinking Requirements】
1. **Situational Understanding**: Analyze key nouns in the user's question (such as 'car wash', 'gas station', 'parking lot', etc.) and understand their implied behavioral scenarios
   - Example: 'car wash' = drive to wash the car, not just go there and look around
   - Example: 'gas station' = drive to refuel, not walk to visit
2. **Purpose Principle**: Users go somewhere to do what should be done there, this is the default premise
   - Go to car wash = need to wash the car (car is the service object, must drive)
   - Go to gas station = need to refuel (car is the service object, must drive)
   - Go to restaurant = need to eat (person is the service object, can walk or drive)
   - Go to supermarket = need to shop (person is the service object, can walk or drive)
3. **Common Sense Judgment**: Analyze whether key information conforms to common sense and real logic
4. **Accurately Understand User Intent**: Don't take things out of context, understand the complete scenario and default premises
5. **Identify Implied Premises**: Many questions have default scenarios that don't need to be explicitly stated by the user

【Thinking Steps】
1. Extract key nouns and verbs from the question
2. Analyze the function of this place: What is the user going there to do? (Default is to do what should be done there)
3. Analyze who the service object is: Is it a person or a car? (Determines what transportation to use)
4. Provide reasonable suggestions based on situational common sense

【Output Format】
Thinking:
1. Keyword Analysis: [Extract key nouns, analyze the function of this place and default behavior]
2. Service Object Judgment: [What is the user going there for? Is the service object a person or a car?]
3. Scenario Understanding: [What is the user's real intent and complete scenario?]
4. Common Sense Judgment: [Based on the purpose principle, analyze whether it conforms to real logic]
End of Thinking
Final Answer: [Give the most reasonable suggestion based on situational common sense]

meta.title: RertChat - AI Assistant
meta.description: Intelligent conversation assistant based on Cloudflare

home.welcomeSubtitle: I can help you answer questions, write code, create content, or just chat

conversations.title: All Conversations

conversations.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this conversation?
conversations.startConversation: Start a conversation with your AI assistant

faq.title: FAQ
faq.contactUsDescription: If you have other questions, feel free to contact us through the following methods
faq.cfEnvironment: RertChat uses Cloudflare Pages environment variables and secret storage to manage API keys, ensuring key security.
faq.browserIndexedDB: Your conversation data is stored in the browser's IndexedDB, which is a local storage space. Data is not uploaded to any server and is completely saved on your device.
faq.clearBrowserData: Yes, if you clear your browser's cache, cookies, or site data, your conversation data will be lost. It is recommended to regularly export conversations as a backup.
faq.syncDevices: Currently the app uses local storage and does not support cross-device synchronization. You can export conversations as JSON files and import them on other devices.
faq.conversationsStoredLocally: Conversation content is only stored in your browser's local storage (IndexedDB) and is not uploaded to any server. AI service providers may retain conversation content for model improvement. Please refer to each service provider's privacy policy.
faq.apiKeySecurityTips: API keys are stored locally in your browser and are only used to call AI services. We recommend:
faq.dontExposeKey: Do not enter or display your keys in public places
faq.dontCommitKey: Do not commit keys to public code repositories
faq.checkNetwork: Check if network connection is normal
faq.checkConsole: Check if there are error messages in the browser console
faq.updateBrowser: Update your browser to the latest version
faq.submitIssueOnGithub: Submit an Issue on GitHub repository
faq.browserRunningBackground: Is the browser running in the background
faq.websiteNotificationEnabled: Is the website notification switch enabled

questions.startNewConversation: How do I start a new conversation?
questions.importConversation: How do I import previous conversations?
questions.exportConversation: How do I export my conversations?
questions.enableDesktopNotification: How do I enable desktop notifications?
questions.noNotificationReceived: Why am I not receiving notifications?
questions.turnOffNotificationSound: How do I turn off notification sound?
questions.dataStorageLocation: Where is my conversation data stored?
questions.clearBrowserData: Will clearing browser data delete my conversations?
questions.syncAcrossDevices: How do I sync conversations across devices?
questions.conversationSaved: Will my conversation content be saved?
questions.messageSendFailed: What should I do if sending messages fails?
questions.pageDisplayIssue: How to resolve page display issues?
questions.feedback: How can I provide feedback or suggestions?

answers.availableShortcuts: Visit the Keyboard Shortcuts Guide page to view the complete list of shortcuts and descriptions.
answers.startNewConversation: Click the + New Conversation button in the top-left corner of the sidebar to start a new conversation. You can also click the New Conversation button on the conversations list page.
answers.importConversation: Go to the Conversations List page, click the Import button, and select the exported JSON file. Note: The imported file must be in the format exported from the Export feature.
answers.exportConversation: On the Conversations List page, you can: Click the export button next to a single conversation to export it; Click the "Export All" button in the top-right corner to export all conversations
answers.apiKeyManagement: RertChat uses Cloudflare Pages environment variables and secret storage to manage API keys, ensuring key security.
answers.enableDesktopNotification: Go to the Settings page and enable the "Desktop Notification" option in the Notification Settings section. The browser will request notification permission when enabled for the first time, please click "Allow".
answers.noNotificationReceived: Please check the following: Is browser notification permission authorized? Is the browser running in the background? Are system notification settings correct? Is the website notification switch enabled?
answers.turnOffNotificationSound: Go to the Settings page and turn off the "Notification Sound" option to disable notification sounds.
answers.dataStorageLocation: Your conversation data is stored in the browser's IndexedDB, which is a local storage space. Data is not uploaded to any server and is completely saved on your device.
answers.clearBrowserData: Yes, if you clear your browser's cache, cookies, or site data, your conversation data will be lost. It is recommended to regularly export conversations as a backup.
answers.syncAcrossDevices: Currently the app uses local storage and does not support cross-device synchronization. You can export conversations as JSON files and import them on other devices.
answers.conversationSaved: Conversation content is only stored in your browser's local storage (IndexedDB) and is not uploaded to any server. AI service providers may retain conversation content for model improvement. Please refer to each service provider's privacy policy.
answers.apiKeySecurity: API keys are stored locally in your browser and are only used to call AI services. We recommend: Do not enter or display your keys in public places; Regularly rotate your keys; Do not commit keys to public code repositories
answers.messageSendFailed: If sending messages fails, please check: Is the network connection normal? Are there error messages in the browser console?
answers.pageDisplayIssue: Please try the following steps: Refresh the page; Clear browser cache; Open in incognito mode; Update your browser to the latest version
answers.feedback: You can provide feedback through: Submit an Issue on GitHub repository; Contact us via email

about.title: About
about.subtitle: RertChat
about.missionDesc1: In this AI era, many services force users to register and login before using them, which not only wastes time but also brings privacy and security risks.
about.missionDesc2: RertChat was born to break this situation. We hope to create a lightweight and convenient AI chat assistant, so that anyone can enjoy the convenience of AI anytime, anywhere, without constraints.
about.value1Desc: Let users start chatting immediately without registration or waiting
about.value2Desc: Users can switch between different AI models anytime to find the best fit
about.value3Desc: Local storage, data stays on your device
about.value4Desc: Fully open code, welcome community participation
about.openSourceDesc: RertChat is an open source project, we believe in the power of open source to make products better

keys.title: API Keys
keys.description: Manage your API keys for accessing RertChat AI services
keys.createKeyDescription: Create a new API key for accessing API services
keys.noKeysHint: Click the button above to create your first API Key
keys.rotateTitle: Rotate Key (Generate new Key and auto copy)
keys.copied: Copied to clipboard! Copy button will disappear in 2 seconds
keys.masked: For security, the Key will be masked and cannot be copied after page refresh
keys.usageGuideDescription: How to use API Key in your projects
keys.externalApiDescription: For external applications, third-party integrations, etc.
keys.internalApiDescription: Only for this website's built-in chat interface, external calls will be rejected
keys.securityTip1: Never commit API Keys to version control systems (like Git)
keys.securityTip2: If a Key is compromised, immediately use the rotate feature to generate a new Key
keys.securityTip3: After rotating, the new Key will be automatically copied and the old Key will be immediately invalidated
keys.securityTip4: Regularly rotate Keys to ensure security
keys.securityTip5: Store API Keys in environment variables in production
keys.securityTip6: Never hardcode API Keys in frontend code

docs.title: Documentation Center
docs.description: RertChat provides a complete RESTful API with streaming output and multi-turn conversation context support
docs.quickStartDescription: Basic steps to use the API
docs.step1Description: Login and visit the keys page to get your personal API Key
docs.step2Description: Authenticate with Bearer Token and send chat requests
docs.chatEndpointDescription: Public chat API endpoint, requires valid API Key for authentication
docs.streamNote: Only streaming output (SSE) is supported. Enable streaming by setting stream: true
docs.messagesDescription: Array of messages with role (user/assistant/system) and content fields
docs.modelDescription: Defaults to @cf/qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b-fp8, supports other Cloudflare AI models
docs.streamDescription: Set to true for streaming output (Server-Sent Events), streaming only
docs.statusNote: Used by frontend to display development server connection status
docs.restingDescription: Development server is closed or unreachable
